* **#TXLEGE**

**" Texas is giving out $5B in loans to build natural gas power plants. Some companies say no thanks,**” Houston Chronicle's **Claire Hao** — “For years, Texas politicians have fretted: What will it take for companies to build more natural gas power plants for the state’s strained power grid?

As it turns out, for a growing number of developers, even the Texas government handing out taxpayer-backed loans is not sufficient.

Four companies have pulled their projects from consideration from the $5 billion [Texas Energy Fund](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eNCDHcRdv524vOoQmRT6HI7WJxAH48hnu0MK_YH-2QxGmzJgg5_f3echGgOr5Rzl3CnnfDCy2WhvrOBVPKav1L8mdM5mKrHPZwexVslGl-Bt1kKnG3ehI-nhpT5cfvCNNXd_bHoApExXXtU2xSmo4eqCJASjR14TUwvuOXFE3unemeETYok07XJIX7iLoCBO5MsPQtbs6hjh7yLNM38ZJ9ipoJd-GheJ2dbZVdo0QaKKel2FIOWgnv2mrz-D2caM4uJPOPZ_KLnubFC8oKC2Kf9NvUz4xj-EQqAnFUhq-4-NYnR3gflZg1njnVTFbB0GdMZXmRWvfg0j588owExjfMG9wHh1WTlulM-rNxrITEgbdlC4tDjV7gLF_JmESeDtvQd2f1pDyDW24g12faCXM10XdSfsJKxAaJ09-BVWP1zbAZOQ7BE9iLCfDOJPJ0ogM5PZGk4scbKYV3VfZ7tXv3W4AaaX287uRTkCsMOpX9VY/4fb/2_LEswg7S7OOetbXWMjeMg/h18/h001.KvvTrzTZQbvyM5ULmPKrCVwUUFn77ttYmtBcX0kXFgo), citing various financial and logistical challenges. Another project [was denied loans](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eNCDHcRdv524vOoQmRT6HI7WJxAH48hnu0MK_YH-2QxGmzJgg5_f3echGgOr5Rzl3Kk0QxwMBJTD4Iwap4WiWJhQ0kEZo_4BDSmYoEIlxd6ff75W-ewDE_KWiPHx0aeKMLcdcFJiqUnWm1ai3JkdWvDNLREWGDHOhdfjfSLmmXMtW6ahOnLUpufC60iF2WGwYmVmC0td63d9WsdzO-k1UfcR00qro6VT8qlhyHm4WIqIbSsVBjTNT1RA4UVnRdq5vCZqwXdygFLnO-KqLpxsi7vRt9aj3ufZj1HnnYby7UWFUDW2SLIQzVC3ggv8IxwmvscBTVXtgOkl5BUVDFXVwK0AtxU1QTws6rzFOsGx4-ecoijR2AyIG32wYntNn2Q-fMjgSuFdruAHy_UOGGZo-zrqkRz-M1v13rPUyb6he3kUban_6YKda0rRR4pgsrW9k7AqA1OPkWxt4dSUkXs--jbbrqCunW3E9ZW5tVuUV0xc_Rxo49aU-c0ncB-wzNkTxQ/4fb/2_LEswg7S7OOetbXWMjeMg/h19/h001.tz3SkUcSxrrYKqhkl_YP-9Q9F9-J1QreeiaKBl-jSNM) last fall after one company listed on the application accused the other sponsoring company of fraud.

In total, nearly a third of new project capacity advanced to the fund’s due diligence review process has left the program..

State lawmakers created the Texas Energy Fund in 2023 and set aside the bulk of the money to give companies low-interest loans towards building new gas-fired generation for the Electric Reliability Council of Texas power grid. The fund [was advertised to voters](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eNCDHcRdv524vOoQmRT6HI7WJxAH48hnu0MK_YH-2QxGmzJgg5_f3echGgOr5Rzl3NBFgZEFnD3Yv3LTGDRlULWr_E3oafecRzKJqRoHrvrvXk6T0P0a9-Dr2AV95sfRUU7tXgAVkfSPtY8DOG5E-MCSfvAqOm4CT8kpXcAwXmx5wYCoKkXc2d5Eww1DUEr17bGZyR3t_ba_-2v7S1zU_r4AER4OOzcVuZv9cIefyUTAu6oJwrB2MjhMLY6EAFITIUKM8mGwaPOUCan0HaymcY5B8gQQvlmMkWL-v3exDNcmMUT-MSx2Ohwep5yAf7fsNW6LxeIPA930Gbf3d5FXSKZlypt3CZ0gD9U2hI9E06WaI9Q0KU2AX5ov44jVpmi92bM1aidZwplYXSgmhkQO18dKr17VaPC0AdZc8zE_zLHfJ8em-oB-SJM7uYEqqL327J55tqOGIb2sjM61gXjGIYxtcWJvNlLGPlBEMouAVMQnW5pPgF7suWK0vD99b0LXkA/4fb/2_LEswg7S7OOetbXWMjeMg/h20/h001.cj4pWh05bdxR_S0rTi5ajcHVUiZ0-ExbGu7N2hrnU3k), who approved the program as a constitutional amendment, as a way to bolster the grid after the February 2021 winter freeze caused statewide outages and [killed hundreds.](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.y1qQg7vX491TJbzrGLYTuPgGHFyizm5vyYLi9R1f5oEGdLinXt98lTCa-Ig3nSPCB3E4I70X87PKi2lIj9sKsAE13r3moLo6ML_FJ2Li0lMmMJB15CpeVjRHDuiD_9kZkRkjPvSUj9jhBla0atyg6VY0NPPab8L3rPebh6Xa_muxGikDi_AgSqKLlj0dUZY4kaRdEb0ERkYhGzpFYDbzUlVcw14UQL8hXyU6gdShafWEjeyBc6Lz10l64wc1oxdeD2qWKf_jPsWv3b9--nqAHv43kWkhtmbJ6NiGoNJ_yLCBzl--f1M62wN8Kr5HzwuZJXgjYBA6T807vTQI51Qt3UAZAZzJnzTE6a4pa5Dq4WqrhQFSPgsIDk5s5hbpkImjl4arDWz7hpApIjrvwBTh7V1jPkWHT2jqUAS4WlUmJrk19zQnE3Yhi35sCEoGK6e9nCLVNufAOURJNbEkaZjO2vkedpLrjmJSeL--KevlGS0/4fb/2_LEswg7S7OOetbXWMjeMg/h21/h001.kA-vi21mRMnRYuLroQXVfZCGIWzoPIS1koh9XC0sTMk)

Then, last year, ERCOT [forecasted an unprecedented increase](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eNCDHcRdv524vOoQmRT6HI7WJxAH48hnu0MK_YH-2QxGmzJgg5_f3echGgOr5Rzl3J7HDCDTjV2wtTNM4kVh2m0uK42_2Ph8Op5kCJWUg5hiBDkNHOgkTawtbmNXn0sABMJftTnOwQ3ifZvchs9lMgf-NS8JuI6LD-fNS0ih2uYfapYECdC12rLHQTiUjf5M7aEenG6vA5xLP2WvCI9rk9TqZEjOnq-GLQAOiD2KE1rIlbfpjP1394qPuaxrtQgYNh6ABMnR_0eWlrFKa4g8iMBMFmTdIfNCMDciLZI-QjginLd1IYIjt6Ehu3vJSh9wdbodbqtEQ7pfbEU6SE_73yDi1mP7TQN87TdDU9OMQwPdcdbZduJq_TrSwaDVPAIWm1l5FqNC3xJAmSFreZhDEqk1gjPGKGwmUakw_cmsxWIddYArhBtRNPGo81lPoNx6CY76jp86Drzf2-wTz9UJuKm7Sp7glmtVQZ-mwqMq_WMb/4fb/2_LEswg7S7OOetbXWMjeMg/h22/h001.fDcmFWKlwNWHdOGP0iUD6S4HhuyuOBVUhBmay103YeY) in electricity demand in the near future as the state’s population and economy grow. Policymakers became even more adamant that Texas needs much more gas-fired power generation for use when wind or solar power isn’t available, even though natural gas is a fossil fuel contributing to climate change.

At first, the Texas Energy Fund seemed popular. [So many companies applied](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eNCDHcRdv524vOoQmRT6HI7WJxAH48hnu0MK_YH-2QxGmzJgg5_f3echGgOr5Rzl3Kk0QxwMBJTD4Iwap4WiWJii0Zgs33M6xCJao5kdzOX1A7TIC-TBFyQH0b93w7h_5yEha87FYAsoghOm9mkMczdqDVjIYWUpU04GSNNp_gUNoTNA5AwPMFg4a0WC3kn2OWmJLMhnluJZikqDK1_Gli3DFq4FMydXOSj5mGyxVUD8GLLf7qsjyq6OfTPQlZnIUBRBuvG5NLBX6CI5L2lIqQl5wHgHGcH8znPpLbHYxMDFKE2sHJF-sjGcXERfe-JUsTeNuQMa1sDq9VblUFH-BxI34czPHTPycTM1_iKA0xPcAoLJnq0c6dYNzamDc2nUZM-v7GMdKQuy_TCrOLklZkE-1NqGlQkm-ircromoz3v-9GYwXNNzX7eOg271-B1SHcIobSr32GGuzZPQ1VcRxBV_k4g40YaXU9lr3lEyEgkytmOr1IyXc4qCTe_ozime9Q/4fb/2_LEswg7S7OOetbXWMjeMg/h23/h001.e-wFDSs6h0qiwGijyihdPcwUCqRws2rvZoacb64-Gik) for loans that state lawmakers — [at the urging](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eNCDHcRdv524vOoQmRT6HI7WJxAH48hnu0MK_YH-2QxGmzJgg5_f3echGgOr5Rzl3Kk0QxwMBJTD4Iwap4WiWJhNRksVHvPWSq1UIvPFCVuNAoYJjvh0SsHaF6y4zN7vrf_nCwoLzcjJYvdogOpIveHe2x62q16nqra_1NAdz6agKQk_CAmPV2YbmJtxGscWR9wm15pk5MxPIups5MX1lDJ_YtCJUMuNBlsjei85_oekzfJpWWc3jb3XHzzvsDkNWP8CulKFn5UxzGhg_d6GmCRaaGGAj9B65OVx84-ws6rXZzevovm_e_C4lkT8UbjXFVBLrFxCKiEdf3Vi4MUamCTaIhg96Ca9vls9Hf4Wxusoksli_j9qcbmmUvJsPTLWALgWCnVLYdFpTihER4l5Ie5_9675PurDaQLOAERupJDCb4RuD1sixY4LdN5OM2zLHkyMwgS-InyB-MMzb2YgWBoTreIi2ZK2EXwmLjgLJu4S/4fb/2_LEswg7S7OOetbXWMjeMg/h24/h001.Qf0SaBRh-K663v9b7w3U6q0dg1bnPrPW5H_xznCSnv0) of Gov. **Greg Abbott** and Lt. Gov. **Dan Patrick** — are considering appropriating another $5 billion to the program in the ongoing legislative session, so that it totals $10 billion.

But critics of the Texas Energy Fund have noted that natural gas power plants, the primary generation source on the ERCOT grid, [suffered the most failures](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eN2L0VZE85CMaIGcWaN6z6LihrDOZqwhdG9IgDLFZnjUxkke0Nec1RsYhFHODohiSKuAF9jYg1luBRHAVEfNXIaJRDlfE9Bns9N4bziZtfbt1bj_2xxytSSkTgV_vUuCyTQFGKDy-TQvREC8BjoLM-DfHXc_iD5ndZwhUDwc_nFmA1IkKE0jakL_WJO2vjptEm1HH5YFEBLpGXztQ-iBmLG7RWWZg9-q3vHD3Ct0PMTePIcw-hlqUrl6KPQGKQTprOzYFN8BrT82koaGmOAx7nzqTo--w7hGWdz3loTjCmF4VDhE6l0r3flJ5etzt0MPE2bB-GL4zundIWDCbTUVq4vvpge6-myCpbKFf_sPVb14bfjqpMl4k6hVZDplhoOdaUTw2RD48Iea7NL6WSlm1PBt0QrL35yIMx18KO38Wv6KfMzIjejDG93raRNcFKthJYQmJ_ymAl67CxDLJHwdPTED7O6Z3JSE9gcFt0IGJiihfLhIUruCeExQ2b-YBCfTJQ/4fb/2_LEswg7S7OOetbXWMjeMg/h25/h001.A4PBIzbx56Iwa77efEc-tbsgDT-_zS6vB2p4mtufpYk) during the 2021 winter storm. [Others have said](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eKoI-qaDO7_arVjGwz3gEM5Dl9bK_9oRSXchYqwa9kSFfDH70fB9FPSmH5SGAwz0tLgGpzofb61mSTIyF7hjmruBfAEIYcrilj3_m4XYUiH5ZqjlJzniSO95e0LZq7PICh4VycapXL0u8Hs3a7wmzaU1enOpUa6BPAv2ujx516WTSp9f6Qs9zHxITkpQU0vAlaai8jqWE9-DkE7Z2LjozlmVq3YjzP5K2AMqvu_D_Jub7jwZ0wRJnQW1LhH0F_SpOzN1NDh-Aagllw1YYl0L5t1vgCoHFd0Nb7_UtJcsM7BcrSZdmrlFII0Q07_hq3QhhbusN_NAlqf0J8jwUsPvCKjE_Jx6gOBsB-Y9ff-0EQ2pZBcMniOYzS90gNMxx4e1u3UUh1_C2-vdVsA4LvM_diXN2STo-rv57i68EHnGHZGR/4fb/2_LEswg7S7OOetbXWMjeMg/h26/h001.6yqmeHS5Hp9SfxtkF3EHmhMlgKftYGWCN9CTWJh0VqE) the Public Utility Commission of Texas, the state agency running the program, doesn’t have enough expertise assessing default risk to manage billions in taxpayer dollars.

**Adrian Shelley** , director of Public Citizen’s Texas office, said the fund’s recent dropouts have validated [the progressive nonprofit’s concerns](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eIgtSAUVGdD0hvE9vY2VTyeaKYBjgz4I0nFhxmLX0vl8EMGiP_guk2xjEKV7GJR8W37S0hE3cnEGuhhpAVX_rU85f7QtmloyoUD3pfnI3kgc1w2K-zTdiS8JxXJMOqISbBv5HL17L418N8iCxY2B0PNdQvHUQDkWLmtncVBbr-BwsYAS6IgVjghQBvXSTsYmcZ3gZhmYK4Z0Q6c6O7rSVByUCTwBYJXzCwRoBlkNJsHt2neODYuakeMz0x54Fs9Ilnq5l8U94tKPyiLY-r4bLoTiYaFfNpIml9QTriaqSkyH2YQ-xFw0EErNuW8R17bNGvEoELvBUCFBCL5bAuxTPyijofK3X9GCv7dO2usBETH6NYRna1WOMTijjAKP5BYvZcgJRbMSnIstvxkTdTUok0K-B6ql3S6n1l3e2TlaHEwtvFpkSTykxNX01vwO0eslOyOK3X1XhNgArlSx-iSQHv_B14lWbJv0mq-KoZz6pIvy/4fb/2_LEswg7S7OOetbXWMjeMg/h27/h001.XeysGijnXH-MARWS6CPpfrpKYoy5DfL98OqigOUbEfU).

“Our skepticism about the Texas Energy Fund is borne out,” Shelley said. “Each time funding for one of these projects is canceled, the applicant says, ‘We're concerned about costs, we're concerned about supply chain, we're concerned about the timeline.’ These are the concerns that we raised when this was being proposed in the legislature.”

In an email statement, PUC spokesperson **Ellie Breed** said 16 Texas Energy Fund applications — totaling more than 8,300 megawatts of potential new generation — are still active and [undergoing due diligence review](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.yT0LsjtC9VeS77VMJuB_4q7m0x1NoVuZFGWJbj-5lHUp5_Qceb--bS-ff0YyN5LVCHvAIxo45Bz5Crd1riCH_py1GqugsDccDfsMxms08GNZrrGCTN5DuEk8nWz4Vl6lsFp8Eati09V4VJgS0Lo0i8aKoR_macNn7lAv6Ixc4OKxF_urDNraMfcD9RLu_vyL6hnNkTM-jFfAKIu7ZfiIIeqeqymerI0NlvCH6mvokxEb2ODBG6f6CYp3dNn3Uj2W1JPz_CU0NtOQWNkzXuaWGqtJkt5xG_qmio-0F45-7hVi-3B0BiDA1ATx47rDiQI50NIowAagezR2Hot9NIUP5ndSngVCL-Co57UtvWLG9iuUvsju4am6OzPFovf5rEHB3Aqnu-4tHukN-8Z-DY6-VqF170bb4-VOYviPaHNa0WUbc81vXyOxNTzpGYmvQYg4fxeNamDZFoQOFD-D7RrTZQ/4fb/2_LEswg7S7OOetbXWMjeMg/h28/h001.bX_JQZp5j3GlOPcz_SPcEhMbb5-jgaOLa5kO1SwhGaA). The agency’s staff plans to recommend another slate of projects for review at an upcoming meeting, she wrote.

“The PUCT continues to pursue the objectives of [all four TEF programs](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eEeIGFHUMxd-e_thAjwJ50sYgX3NDZPUlNyg-XM7B4BYdDNpj-th1HC861x9PaMMD_ZpxsFRJ-UBYoZ4B2ULRGeR5W5yXK9_CR46qLmK-ipjwujleJS8N9CkfkfzkiC1O4tYY2LPNqyzcFb1ZdDdH3ejLFbOHj2iXS4Nkg829C-05weKTRNk9sJ-lLCUy5gzqHJTa5OjxW8eeyZHRJOUkrF6KK_1Mij0Mr3v7tIXfIpvMvvy0uUb9jPjH5pdfp68mqFMQpD91sZ1uydlu7dFb42-H9iaBJ6IsQjTbJFvuTgb2pU0iLZEmtqPk9qKnUvGFqRoFJ_YRabtJzZXP5q0SnYh3YcWaxQhg6MQ2q6Adg5XPV-FVMymR5C9S_BPIRzCKZVgSFMYskBPheVYQ9VA0ns/4fb/2_LEswg7S7OOetbXWMjeMg/h29/h001._hQ7Gur04ByvpTWZ4jEixjuK3bxynUUEpy8aPo4REiQ), while ensuring the appropriate use of the taxpayer dollars dedicated to fulfilling those goals,” Breed wrote." **" Matthew McConaughey, Woody Harrelson testify in support of Texas film incentives bill,**” Fox 7 Austin's **Rudy Koski** — “There was some Hollywood star power in Austin on Monday.

Actors**Matthew McConaughey** and **Woody Harrelson** testified before the Senate Finance Committee in support of legislation to beef up an incentive plan for the entertainment industry.

Lockhart became a film set back in July when the TV western series "1923" came to town. The production was one of several entertainment projects lured to Texas with money from an incentive program.

On Monday, state Sen. **Joan Huffman** (R-Houston) pitched legislation to revamp the program.

"For every dollar paid in a grant, $4.69 is spent in the state, according to the office of the governor," said Huffman.

Senate Bill 22 pumps $500 million every two years into the new Texas Moving Image Incentive Program. The grant money comes with several strings attached. There is bonus money for using rural areas and a rejection clause for scripts that put Texas in a negative light.

"It further codifies a list of projects that are currently not eligible for reimbursement, such as: pornography or obscene material, political advertising, casino-type video games, and more," said Huffman.

Central Texas plays a big role in a lot of movies and TV productions. The City of Lockhart took on a new role for a popular TV show '1923', a prequel to the 'Yellowstone' series.

The committee hearing featured a unique cast of supporting characters that included Lt. Gov. **Dan Patrick**. SB 22 is a priority bill for Patrick, but his attendance was overshadowed by actors **Matthew McConaughey** and **Woody Harrelson**.

Earlier this year, the two teamed up with fellow Texan **Dennis Quaid** and met with the Lt. Governor. The meeting came after the three released a promotional movie trailer for the incentive program.

In the video, Quaid suggested a "Wholesale takeover." Harrelson responded by saying, "A small fraction of the Texas budget surplus could turn this state into the new Hollywood."

In the committee hearing, the Texas story pitch came with a plot twist when McConaughey testified.

"So, I'm going to propose a bigger thought here. I'm going to propose doubling down on, if we pass this bill, what we can do. How do we do that? Number one, we train our own workforce. We partner with local community colleges and tech schools across the state of Texas, and we get Texan men and women trained and certified to learn the below the line trade jobs needed to make films," said McConaughey.

Lawmakers were told that local communities could be prepared for the way jobs programs are done for more traditional industries.

"We have a real pipeline. We have infrastructure, we have wood screws, not Velcro," said McConaughey.

Doing that, McConaughey suggested, would make Texas a permanent supporting cast member. The grant money, with the infrastructure, would exceed what's being done by Georgia and New Mexico.

It was even argued that eventually, providing a financial incentive may no longer be necessary.

"This is how Texas becomes its own profitable and self-sustaining industry. This is how Texas becomes more than just a destination for out-of-state productions. This is how Texas becomes more than just a tax-incentivized advertisement, with hey, come shoot here. If we do this, we begin to create our own industry. We do this, we start to create our own pipeline of film and television, which, in turn, means producers and financiers are not going to want to go to other states because they don't have the infrastructure that we'll have, and they're too expensive," said McConaughey.

**Chad Gundersen** , a producer for the TV series "The Chosen", also spoke to the committee. He agreed with McConaughey that the new infrastructure system should also include post-production work.

Gunderson noted how Texas A&M; has a great animation program, but a lot of the Aggie graduates leave Texas to find work." **" At ERCOT's request, CPS spending $150M to speed up new transmission lines,**” San Antonio Express-News' S**ara DiNatale** — “[CPS Energy](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eOGN1bwhOhMtnLUti-SktFbAjhBvc66kuL47moCUzG3FSuKHw36fegOhTfwdRMcVs202Fvz8AN4-JyaP7u5jUBmKyLtbLtS8ay43CD-qGvsVC3IcBGktcKpgMEqbv_GikUoYhTfeqW6tVGm2am1nsIEpbGQfIpoo9lQDl998_Gc507pi6QE5pUGEZKLaSuch9cqLfFjYGZuKxOJLT14yGX9k8tDWKl8gg-adclPXApZSUn2kLHslL13m__xyTNvq9kIISpJoHXm5-Yix_Q-d5ynoMlHBU0DT4EdDE_VFgSAEcOKqxuvmnCoxvLvH_geEtdTCrxjYQ8CYHRWgRW_ChA5TN0Kssa3X3fkqRK34I6xrTERkQ9PS5GVp-k-oe8TJXHOH_CguOFxorPBlMEs00djTyR7vo6VG67JDF6AX1atB32paSPLFtLcY_VpthF6TkA/4fb/2_LEswg7S7OOetbXWMjeMg/h32/h001.08je5jtmSjhsvIXhjeLckdfV3FQ8Jl31xMYozpsxhSY) is spending another $150 million to speed up construction of transmission lines the state grid operator says will reduce the risk of blackouts across Texas.

A [troubled circuit](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eLA4v3xJ7EFyLSWgpDk4sY8ZxH9AT_caGIyjyacIkJhSMw-EtmvdaP5K5_x52-4IeOz3g1wwoRJBeE2MDeQRNfA648GlH-hg1BIobDsUl2M8oO7dnzB523J4Ye1EgnolEZ_QJDguI8bcDZocJ6LaycF-DGOWVMRG_-EmhQEURFMcwW49CzXKoAk173AUjKfH3Uhen0udOl4QqYT-ZyA-giZDCaxVUag80bdQfi7cXxxTuF2fQaotOd_YEj9i8l-c_FLoFiaD62e9ZoBm9H_iQ3EfDEgP8jfOc0NopzSodbFsuD0ZPHmsfJKuFEj8NJ2TsQaO0UM2mtVQZrYaS7WKx81A_x6OIvkbHlYVwKnYpbsktNf5vsYUFshI7TjgFwi9Lv5_CkWSR0EuoCvuls1Q_E0LXQJcAHJsxK3sPU5Qre6mbBjtGe22dmvthwHxkAylrN-gsEIKu-mA-PD143G_4_jV7wB3B7JQn09cFJU_aOvc/4fb/2_LEswg7S7OOetbXWMjeMg/h33/h001.2JKu9lCRZPjTbjHluxzKww-I2bEXXK3wjkXi8S8fMyk) near San Antonio will be getting relief years sooner than originally planned at the behest of the Electric Reliability Council of Texas, which operates the statewide grid. The project is adding 58 miles of new transmission lines starting at the city-owned utility’s J.K. Spruce power plant’s substation and running through South Texas.

Most of the cost, which now totals $585 million, will be paid by utility customers across the state.

But the vendor contract the CPS board approved Monday isn’t simple. ERCOT’s desire to accelerate the work meant CPS had to find a company willing to do the work while the lines remain energized and actively moving electricity.

“You basically put on a suit that energizes yourself at the same what they call ‘potential’ as that line, so that you can work on it safely,” said CPS President and CEO **Rudy Garza**. “I’ve done it. It’s a lot scarier putting the suit on than it is actually putting your hands on the line. But, in essence, you’re working at the same potential — the same voltage environment as the transmission line.”

Garza said there was only one company able and willing to take on the project — Houston-based [Quanta Services](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.StuO2a1wIVm3IQmILidXB2Rs-2VsYk8d2DM8uy6g0mR8dNB23VQ1GboM3kLSp-1eKTGdpm_OroV2WmD4kg5tmgixzcF_Q6FyTcMSPvCawF6j3fbz0Ar1OFS_KHJi-olQpqWwNWXuR2aQ-EuXV1-X3OyZ8JcvHRocNVundLt2R1N2SH4YFbWxLTZ5DwkOsjcmE7jy7egMonRNZYe0ZvblrjwoktHrms2TOVhtSVpQX8ULUUQYjXGSDMX4oTUnZKq4-mreEWdICI5WalpJtfpAYUKodFHdN7pIYdVbKDxE4yJKTHf7UFRESWikm2AkET3kPNqPwONKRc4zZgKKRmGFgpxRayadMi8-B7gsaJfo5AR2NnnG1-3mXr5EeAYIQQmkvxCQp_e_AHQ_nX-UnzphMQ/4fb/2_LEswg7S7OOetbXWMjeMg/h34/h001.dP_Az0Kj-CI-3QsiHdBvNpax0gjeMDWFSsNDkZEHmN8). “They have a unique skill set to package a number of their companies together to be able to deliver that line,” Garza said of Quanta.

The complete circuit, which makes a ring around San Antonio, is at risk of failure when plentiful power from South Texas wind farms and batteries is needed in North Texas, where capacity hasn’t kept up with increasing demand. Overloading the circuit to meet that demand could prompt grid-wide cascading outages." **" Houston clocks record overtime pay for police, fire as city contends with $330M deficit,**” Houston Chronicle's **Abby Church** — “Despite Houston’s efforts to fill vacancies in its [Solid Waste Management](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eNCDHcRdv524vOoQmRT6HI51J14spDzdVoLQq-qj1UyJBswJpOaff44TDsCA_t8uDuyPJ4M71oM38czdfGmQujzDbRp4ldujl7hPCclmoxmF32rvme0zPE8DjQl3CCRLGU_9dT2pXneOWYXGnyKdXWuMRD46-90DVIYa9Rm3OOAkcX1yhtqXu8kIhjHnyKE9FIOEhG4YB-cmR0EKtMxBbqnJ9sveLsPX8KB5j7ja6wpMKJtJ3hJ5O_VK_SvwoK4369YYosvhdxiPWzS_SP4Z1YFOCtVaWRzjSEQvLxgH-6oYNf5pEzV4KsZlmcgm6LOYF2odkOCCczq1r7_ZYpjm8VWpw2u7VrhG5bgmVFEmIPAadqd78INkWDS1vc0XeWHDdlbTCjpRSLMol1LzNb-Rz0vX6U-1xdkHK39ad4SQLQko6xLEYChnihvIzla7qU3_HMlEiT7tSoGHryXqrdEKFjtdNM2mJmUJLuv4Mgn7y9pZ/4fb/2_LEswg7S7OOetbXWMjeMg/h38/h001.A3o5X6irs0xVKQ8MvF08ppyqyfRY4ked8HfL7UcEBvo) and [public safety departments](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eNCDHcRdv524vOoQmRT6HI43Rg55yAp-yJ6gTrq7xIMLbKdZwMEZi396jewVC_ieRBK7eNsEcQoPTfkyhjDEsFKn5esDjMwsg91vxNZst3XPG3RCQM1BvxUQrZAVk-O_VxlmYpIqsyE4WRqVjLFqSwgMgstjIFgmDwbXi-dgsBMS1Q4fEjmmsylPJyWoX9YhgSMsciSERa3BkWJSmEFnRq6lnFd2PoWasFzVcFN_TKCuXLUy_8ZgIff74y7ugOOHyBPNg4AtFqjfbdPbipXsTHN_XfHh0yHdN53j9PthUTIGY14ZziDIyBCYryVp8hCXij416_6lQPPdNvvnKsO76mdnkT5QhgqytiazP7owWiGmHU4PE1q8nWCGuzzrLo_VYJABkFoCVjRUaBk06csGAsA1bAoTTg0dipxSsMfQ_HHznHaZkQ1__suEe4cSQhgv1QT0YKNNa90CBP6PdlwZNQ8-Lth4HTXgBc2ndGlfacBLmaJPAVeHkfwtlEyr7Z6lqw/4fb/2_LEswg7S7OOetbXWMjeMg/h39/h001.6lsf1PfAzJr6Ou-sBg203a7Oni_RwJOYyWKR9BO2-HI), city leaders continue to shell out more and more cash to pay their employees overtime.

As Houston contends with a [$330 million budget deficit](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eNCDHcRdv524vOoQmRT6HI51J14spDzdVoLQq-qj1UyJBswJpOaff44TDsCA_t8uDszutw6hbDmGTfiofwiiAmqGfjWIN0FIwfDXAAui6pFd0Lqc1i02sidxHj2MRe7hQL4zGgABF4USDSEfyhdQI-Dwa-0Dn8prnI9K2vjF7bz-e6DD5cZCnja4heSGbRTlfJfjBf_mNWE0pBW1RQePaLTuNuJoIZAHuBXf1RT1ZKWaxkWPsJy63XdVPTtHT_noyDkGiLmXD55wcNIYxlO6j5w2RdWJCcjW9FuroEd8BSHQ2r31gXFVHDVm-CGZ-zGkAGLU4Jc4c4jcWvEB16GSZU79eMyfi96VtmotWQ_r9PGvTvWEyLm0xgis0vn5nCmvlzrKqHkqh9dJoj86_Vw1u5OkiTTo4CsO2Ctk1OPTEDHEtHLF7gnDEBfcmMWomVf1HcYUnrIgdbkCL023im6K0QIxFjsTdYvdDoIBLHSqDfQw/4fb/2_LEswg7S7OOetbXWMjeMg/h40/h001.8mrDLext130L_le_Bx5iLMrBL87s820AQveWFQf7ZVE) heading into the next fiscal year, top officials with the controller’s office said Tuesday that Houston had [broken a record in paying its employees over their standard workweeks](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eEl4MAKW26Ob2hvGxAlssTt9VdJ0OBae5ERArty8wC1FStFe5wdL0Qv4XzkqcwX2k41HKfsW_gcrepxj1-in-UTKxC37P0TrPq16j7AzBTxVFGW3WYhBqXRVooXr5kX5OJrfZzxSD-6Eh_JR7tK49dmFibABrCxccDt8oTdAzK5AHW9esKaGUZ07ORl8-JTUeCGVKAt2AmI09CWkeZJSDGycWXCUhnchbN_azJGJYut2JRjMcsyNWb9Hr-FEyyO81ujqY0D_qaU3QiqEL-5e8Y0tHJIwlL36Epf5mwNIEDFg-sVNV58zGfWml3kbuu1NkZ-MhUgRi9b0yYMDIMBJI-vf_19Wt7YrmHutY4a20MRq_d6EAsVJ2hw0QzcJxTTCWAzeWltjyoA5JW5jxaVCpa2Wmlw-TOXMhgcM_ret8FMQsLvNAGBxq6GwpP-5myTKo_4z27AF3Y71Q4sTAsygmqE/4fb/2_LEswg7S7OOetbXWMjeMg/h41/h001.Oe2_IY_1noq9uIEFwkjpJN3VSYuNOHGcpUWcgYuoZ8g).

The city budgeted around $65 million for employee overtime in last year’s budget, a figure that represents 4% of the city’s personnel budget.

Houston is on track to spend more than double the budgeted amount, and may consequentially spend close to $137 million on overtime alone before the end of the fiscal year, Deputy Controller Will Jones told council members at Tuesday’s Budget and Fiscal Affairs meeting.

The city’s solid waste, police and fire departments have all taken several steps to hire more personnel, from increasing pay to adding another police cadet class to passing a historic deal with the fire union that includes $650 million in back pay. Even so, overtime pay continues to increase.

Some council members listened to the presentation Tuesday with furrowed brows, and Mayor Pro Tem **Martha Castex-Tatum** referred to the information as “bleak.”

Council Member **Sallie Alcorn** , the committee’s chair, had only one remark after she thanked Jones for the presentation.

“I learned a lot,” she said. “My stomach hurts.”

The three culprits of increased overtime spending are Houston’s Solid Waste Management, Fire and Police departments.

According to data presented Tuesday, the police department was budgeted $13.7 million in overtime pay, but is on track to spend nearly $40 million – a 190% increase over its budget.

The fire department, while budgeted for $45.3 million in overtime, is on track to spend $88 million – a 94% increase over budget.

While Solid Waste was only budgeted $4 million in overtime, the department is on track to instead spend $7.1 million – 75% over its budget, according to data presented Tuesday.

Truck operators were the most frequent users of overtime, and the position happens to be the hardest to fill at the department, Jones said. The overtime use at Solid Waste comes despite increased spending on contracting outside vendors to pick up trash and adding new vehicles. In 2024, missed trash pickup was the [second most complained about issue](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eNCDHcRdv524vOoQmRT6HI51J14spDzdVoLQq-qj1UyJBswJpOaff44TDsCA_t8uDkqyZ9m7MxL9mUkTowJNFuI7UwgI-UBPN78OfTyfVviMCK3XtaedQ7YU2LVzCUYBdvHejh0oCqqayWAOErGhwNGDJ-B6LyyNJOHpgb30sgHBNQX2W0XASxlHVST9gAxF3OkyCEIy7rAsCcVpHvRYg9YPMWGt_v4JO2m2c1mk9GadP_Ns68v4NkbwhXUVAMxXhGEoIdmNcOenxxCx9Av11qhGCaRZ5N3A4GhkZx4Z1ZDPgBDK_tz4IJtYdnwpgDDIBjg05wCToLsR4-HICqGtbLlzYeZZAqBawHymifjVqxdH0GXUuQlh6EfEnScj2gJ1vXae0ll4Xf2VL9I8AmLcDf6c207zzd4Dwq-SSbPOWiZ35doIRm8fqA2UnkN7ZxzUyX0_xNpEgGYXJxcPGsPfUXSLHz6wdhGhbHiUc33zpY-u/4fb/2_LEswg7S7OOetbXWMjeMg/h42/h001.89esaDAJDQ3WvetmrQe7HJV6eoEQvLxvkBqfQNQluWg) citywide.

An analysis of the fire and police department’s top earners showed firefighters were on average making 231% of their pay from overtime alone, while police officers made 118% of their pay from overtime. The same analysis for Solid Waste showed top earners made on average 91% of their salary from overtime.

All three departments were impacted by spikes in overtime during the derecho in May, Hurricane Beryl and January’s winter storm, but even without those natural disasters, all three departments would still be over their yearly overtime budgets, Jones said.

* **2026**

**“Tarrant County Democratic Party lays off all paid staff, declines to share details,** ” Fort Worth Report's**Cecilia Lenzen** — "Tarrant County Democratic Party Chair **Crystal Gayden** said Tuesday that her party is strategically restructuring, although she declined to share details on what that restructuring looks like.

**Lon Burnam** , a Democrat who represented Tarrant County in the Texas House between 1997 and 2015 and remains involved with the party, confirmed that the party has laid off its three paid staff positions. The layoffs were first made public in the [Lone Star Left](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eH-DdPEz66jULA4FWFd3EL_oJ1id_S3RRzEd78VNvQ3rG9VrfkxaTo7g9swj_DbTJSOuYFRbnJPCDjOMYeUesq-IBwImTzs25xMLH4OO_-T5bwaj--9IVMk-2EZGAIbo93zaE4ehv1MoCppb1h2pzN0DyCn3vkX-egRakZVkNJHG7gVMQZy1Ezhm_w4RQgXCsqMmL2UBqDJCHWRUYAwbrpghoisd2f0lJ3B0fyPn8WseSD_c0sf1yIWbOHY7x1S06CwZE1UqDxwvPubje_vzoB8d4CmlUCL9CW1dOt908qP_WqZ6VIQR-rkR-QNbEZrsX-VayU4sZcNpi1FfXOy2_vwO3vxCyQfTA1f7OQ_8bE-NWHbjOny60WUDlWMUIa_duFzJ7cDKZF-3GXlE63qT_vW6_nMnzqBrDB2H1-8Uuq70I2gccFDb564NqhDs76MDjQ/4fb/2_LEswg7S7OOetbXWMjeMg/h44/h001.9Cv6Ud1ntvo6AQXFlyK0KFUXfEcBP1l9Qgick7etEE4) newsletter.

Gayden declined to confirm how many staffers were laid off, when the layoffs were implemented or how they stand to impact the party. She said the party is currently focused on [fighting gerrymandering and a redistricting effort](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eGiz_tO-Lxj4aX7ZcoIJMqTgTtm-1B8XVmcH05aJRlhwbCUd-iMseHrI9GjHzCjGajQGdH-iF7DrB-U31Xh7NTRK2Vr3Mr0-dsbiFTihgor6BnWGBTmBfIvn4dRV0qtMvGz9ckfrRii5JLHQY0ELr_tiv_bnTf0KL0LTg4mu31UXvcvLBoUsN6G-HdEH06-7kVhsIIvFK7nlQa-7bA2dS4EDAbMXQGSaagpwBpA7ebA1L53LfPPpE_JWWPfwD6n_bdyQvWqWUxXTHUGlLtCuCx0glI7jm9EylzXlB93anjUGMyr22wdEjxRfabS0jQzG-D_EX4HpuMBm1sOknd7TDkFZ716XigWN6E9pVfB2OMHFHrOOEU3k1vMiKbFlTFSQ9hURe0P7MQEytXOJN-G9tgxXdplRMY2daFdobsEAYLO_mODLpIOobTFz2o8LwYj61A/4fb/2_LEswg7S7OOetbXWMjeMg/h45/h001.bRtyjAgiW_yy2y7OrKfVr5Yh5zRmWLgCvPympQh0sgo) in the Tarrant County Commissioners Court.

“The Tarrant County Democratic Party’s recent restructuring is a strategic decision, not a financial crisis,” Gayden, an attorney and previous judicial candidate who has served as chair since 2023, said in a statement to the Report. “It is common for parties to adjust staffing between election cycles.”

Several other leaders within the party declined to comment, referring questions to Gayden.

The layoffs come one month before the [May 3 election](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.vPsDTRRm1cA9m3Oa8MsMcNPW7q6zFOW4jQi-NdCeP6JizsSX82IeWYSjQRMhYiIM3_TES-S4w6J_5lkjSVZz3w1BlVMk2YmZVtb9ihAeS7qbqPflNfIOH12yfLtqs3sbQgsUXSdSDOL7YPb05ZtuYKrpdIxlzthFNjmyPnWlD5xRKMHY9chptQL0FkTOm0myCWcyU_lBnVfua70zg0dvWVFEfaWSXgMouDfvlGflKimvHr7hxSrVq0BrXCvNhFYsLHWj0uJBlGaFzjv5DBwLGseCDGfJrwRWPi3X9Johw1ywkMLxWe26LzSTfPsPV-FsFYieWXXIb4jZhrDAKE_y2YuxtibAs84PUURYr1A3_UmnisH4yt8G_kYiCC4lJ8Mgf1v-nmG5ctiM-XCe9Pd011iGE7bp0Qc-HudtEYZQGb0EV54UV_BerUnHBVBjl4QwQa8dh31QFIorMDi4WTzvsXOgyGXvWx3EJzZ2HkdrfliNpDFI8is68qR3hNgodUC48CoaVP4HtTBYT8VtfTwjpA/4fb/2_LEswg7S7OOetbXWMjeMg/h46/h001.GOylEUwtAPUbG2n69L6xs5EkxKhp2QdlECsU8Z0Bhpc), when City Council, school board and other local races will be on the ballot across Tarrant County. Local races are all nonpartisan positions, but both the county’s Democrat and Republican parties have become increasingly involved in recent years.

Burnam said he’s disappointed by the layoffs but noted that it’s common for political parties to struggle with fundraising after a presidential election.

“Let’s be clear that it is always the case that after any presidential election, it is hard to raise money for either party — but it’s particularly hard if you lose,” Burnam said.

President **Joe Biden** narrowly won Tarrant County in the 2020 presidential election, becoming the first Democrat to do so since **Lyndon B. Johnson’s** victory in 1964. The county [reverted to red](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.vPsDTRRm1cA9m3Oa8MsMcNPW7q6zFOW4jQi-NdCeP6KdUFG4IIa2PSwEPQ3qzCpMQfVgeW4qJic3A8uNnUhEhCqSR7TRcx3DkiRhJWvAMBjG9PgA1JPcdFWgvrc5B0BBrBT_Y_06BLpfiP04KOq9vASTmySrrpCC72sjk-MFoZCD71_J59UEK1RmJFaIHD-CYcxqcTIetU3NeijaeJhzkvvax81zEjRiLKKIuf1lF1q6RqqMQUtOlW45YbMxR6i3B9eN_0vXRkN4MW4B_NzcNIokMgxr6tyoEJNto8-XHZFuCVShLScwfiGKjU_HXmCZG-50Nkuob6V4rkCEnIV2s47KztuA-_szuomgDC6DgdXieSakxhgAW5CylF_P6wSg79j8L_89DkOJmuwpsW2nI4Rwp-zSWN2vXeuXCc9VXGm1cC_IjuNwhz7lDSmsCkBiVajCY3t1Uwd7hrclCRpDV-fFa7SGo5lwdOUiQQV9tK7X748Mze2fytdIatjzVpxwXhNIL_RRTdxZEP65E2mL6BqX22947tTGlTZejhpjgEY/4fb/2_LEswg7S7OOetbXWMjeMg/h47/h001.Wr_cBoHvsJpg8MCV5mIwT2r8-BGJcbpTjOyetWBap4o) in 2024, supporting President Donald Trump with 51.82% of the vote.

Tarrant Republicans are celebrating the layoffs as a victory. **Bo French** , chair of the Tarrant County Republican Party, was outspoken before and after the November election about his goal to make the county “inhospitable to Democrats” through more involvement in local races. In response to the layoffs Tuesday, French posted on X that his “plan is working.”

In an emailed statement to the Report, French said the Democratic Party’s members “continue to take the 20% position on 80-20 issues.”

“It’s no wonder they are having trouble raising money,” he said." Read the most recent FREE subscriber email . Become a PAID subscriber for $5/mo or $50/yr . | PLEASE NOTE : You are receiving the PAID subscriber version of Must Read Texas — FOR TODAY ONLY — so you can see what you are missing. » Become a PAID subscriber and receive full story summaries and no ads for $7/mo or $70/yr here — . | TOP NEWS “More Houstonians are staying home at night and moving neighborhoods to avoid crime, poll says ,” Houston Chronicle'sMatt deGrood — “Around 77% of Houston survey-takers say they are staying home more at night to avoid crime and 51% said they’d moved homes in search of a safer neighborhood, according to the results of a studying how those living in the country’s biggest cities perceive crime. The survey results come amid a nationwide decline in most types of violent crime after a spike during the coronavirus pandemic. and a decline in homicides, though violent crime ticked up about 5%. “My conclusion is the public perception of crime is much more complex than I think we’d ever thought,” said Ron Faucheux , the analyst that directed the polls. » As part of the project, Faucheux led scientific polls in Houston, New York City, Philadelphia, Phoenix and Chicago – the country’s six biggest cities – and one on a national level, asking people a wide range of crime-related questions specific to the city. Evan F. Trestman Philanthropies, led by a New Orleans-based attorney who became interested in understanding crime after his grandfather and father were murdered 20 years apart, provided $300,000 for the project Marc Levin , the Houston-based chief policy counsel for the Council on Criminal Justice, said he finds the work interesting because it does something unique: In a world full of data about crime, the polls attempt to quantify the less tangible feelings people have about the issue. » “This really exposes some of the limits of the crime data that we do have,” he said. People often make judgments based on less analytical measures, such as homicide numbers. “If someone decides to walk alone in their neighborhood at night, what is the risk? ...Often times, with quality of life stuff, I think they’re looking around at shorthand symbols that they think signal how safe or dangerous an area is. The overwhelming majority of respondents in Houston said they were more careful about avoiding unsafe places, that they were staying home more at night and that they favored laws that would keep criminals in prison longer, according to the poll. While worrying about crime, a slight majority of the same Houston residents polled, around 54%, were either very satisfied or somewhat satisfied with the service from the Houston Police Department, records show. When polled on proposals to improve the agency, improving mental health and addiction treatment facilities received the most enthusiastic support, with around 55% strongly supporting it. Other proposals with more than 50% signaling strong support included improving the agency’s capacity to investigate and solve crimes, increasing the use of computer analytics to solve crimes and expanding career counseling and recreational programs for young people. Increasing patrol manpower and police pay both received slightly less than 50% - 44% and 46% respectively – of people saying they strongly support them, but an overall majority of more than 80% said they'd support it, records show." ($) | PLEASE NOTE : You are receiving the PAID subscriber version of Must Read Texas — for TODAY ONLY. Paid subscribers receive full story summaries, not just headlines and links, which helps them SAVE TIME and be BETTER INFORMED. » Become a PAID subscriber: | | $7/mo Thanks! | #TXLEGE " Texas is giving out $5B in loans to build natural gas power plants. Some companies say no thanks,” Houston Chronicle's Claire Hao — “For years, Texas politicians have fretted: What will it take for companies to build more natural gas power plants for the state’s strained power grid? As it turns out, for a growing number of developers, even the Texas government handing out taxpayer-backed loans is not sufficient. Four companies have pulled their projects from consideration from the $5 billion , citing various financial and logistical challenges. Another project last fall after one company listed on the application accused the other sponsoring company of fraud. In total, nearly a third of new project capacity advanced to the fund’s due diligence review process has left the program.. State lawmakers created the Texas Energy Fund in 2023 and set aside the bulk of the money to give companies low-interest loans towards building new gas-fired generation for the Electric Reliability Council of Texas power grid. The fund , who approved the program as a constitutional amendment, as a way to bolster the grid after the February 2021 winter freeze caused statewide outages and Then, last year, ERCOT in electricity demand in the near future as the state’s population and economy grow. Policymakers became even more adamant that Texas needs much more gas-fired power generation for use when wind or solar power isn’t available, even though natural gas is a fossil fuel contributing to climate change. At first, the Texas Energy Fund seemed popular. for loans that state lawmakers — of Gov. Greg Abbott and Lt. Gov. Dan Patrick — are considering appropriating another $5 billion to the program in the ongoing legislative session, so that it totals $10 billion. But critics of the Texas Energy Fund have noted that natural gas power plants, the primary generation source on the ERCOT grid, during the 2021 winter storm. the Public Utility Commission of Texas, the state agency running the program, doesn’t have enough expertise assessing default risk to manage billions in taxpayer dollars. Adrian Shelley , director of Public Citizen’s Texas office, said the fund’s recent dropouts have validated . “Our skepticism about the Texas Energy Fund is borne out,” Shelley said. “Each time funding for one of these projects is canceled, the applicant says, ‘We're concerned about costs, we're concerned about supply chain, we're concerned about the timeline.’ These are the concerns that we raised when this was being proposed in the legislature.” In an email statement, PUC spokesperson Ellie Breed said 16 Texas Energy Fund applications — totaling more than 8,300 megawatts of potential new generation — are still active and . The agency’s staff plans to recommend another slate of projects for review at an upcoming meeting, she wrote. “The PUCT continues to pursue the objectives of , while ensuring the appropriate use of the taxpayer dollars dedicated to fulfilling those goals,” Breed wrote." ($) " Matthew McConaughey, Woody Harrelson testify in support of Texas film incentives bill,” Fox 7 Austin's Rudy Koski — “There was some Hollywood star power in Austin on Monday. ActorsMatthew McConaughey and Woody Harrelson testified before the Senate Finance Committee in support of legislation to beef up an incentive plan for the entertainment industry. Lockhart became a film set back in July when the TV western series "1923" came to town. The production was one of several entertainment projects lured to Texas with money from an incentive program. On Monday, state Sen. Joan Huffman (R-Houston) pitched legislation to revamp the program. "For every dollar paid in a grant, $4.69 is spent in the state, according to the office of the governor," said Huffman. Senate Bill 22 pumps $500 million every two years into the new Texas Moving Image Incentive Program. The grant money comes with several strings attached. There is bonus money for using rural areas and a rejection clause for scripts that put Texas in a negative light. "It further codifies a list of projects that are currently not eligible for reimbursement, such as: pornography or obscene material, political advertising, casino-type video games, and more," said Huffman. Central Texas plays a big role in a lot of movies and TV productions. The City of Lockhart took on a new role for a popular TV show '1923', a prequel to the 'Yellowstone' series. The committee hearing featured a unique cast of supporting characters that included Lt. Gov. Dan Patrick. SB 22 is a priority bill for Patrick, but his attendance was overshadowed by actors Matthew McConaughey and Woody Harrelson. Earlier this year, the two teamed up with fellow Texan Dennis Quaid and met with the Lt. Governor. The meeting came after the three released a promotional movie trailer for the incentive program. In the video, Quaid suggested a "Wholesale takeover." Harrelson responded by saying, "A small fraction of the Texas budget surplus could turn this state into the new Hollywood." In the committee hearing, the Texas story pitch came with a plot twist when McConaughey testified. "So, I'm going to propose a bigger thought here. I'm going to propose doubling down on, if we pass this bill, what we can do. How do we do that? Number one, we train our own workforce. We partner with local community colleges and tech schools across the state of Texas, and we get Texan men and women trained and certified to learn the below the line trade jobs needed to make films," said McConaughey. Lawmakers were told that local communities could be prepared for the way jobs programs are done for more traditional industries. "We have a real pipeline. We have infrastructure, we have wood screws, not Velcro," said McConaughey. Doing that, McConaughey suggested, would make Texas a permanent supporting cast member. The grant money, with the infrastructure, would exceed what's being done by Georgia and New Mexico. It was even argued that eventually, providing a financial incentive may no longer be necessary. "This is how Texas becomes its own profitable and self-sustaining industry. This is how Texas becomes more than just a destination for out-of-state productions. This is how Texas becomes more than just a tax-incentivized advertisement, with hey, come shoot here. If we do this, we begin to create our own industry. We do this, we start to create our own pipeline of film and television, which, in turn, means producers and financiers are not going to want to go to other states because they don't have the infrastructure that we'll have, and they're too expensive," said McConaughey. Chad Gundersen , a producer for the TV series "The Chosen", also spoke to the committee. He agreed with McConaughey that the new infrastructure system should also include post-production work. Gunderson noted how Texas A&M; has a great animation program, but a lot of the Aggie graduates leave Texas to find work." | STATE GOVERNMENT " At ERCOT's request, CPS spending $150M to speed up new transmission lines,” San Antonio Express-News' Sara DiNatale — “ is spending another $150 million to speed up construction of transmission lines the state grid operator says will reduce the risk of blackouts across Texas. A near San Antonio will be getting relief years sooner than originally planned at the behest of the Electric Reliability Council of Texas, which operates the statewide grid. The project is adding 58 miles of new transmission lines starting at the city-owned utility’s J.K. Spruce power plant’s substation and running through South Texas. Most of the cost, which now totals $585 million, will be paid by utility customers across the state. But the vendor contract the CPS board approved Monday isn’t simple. ERCOT’s desire to accelerate the work meant CPS had to find a company willing to do the work while the lines remain energized and actively moving electricity. “You basically put on a suit that energizes yourself at the same what they call ‘potential’ as that line, so that you can work on it safely,” said CPS President and CEO Rudy Garza. “I’ve done it. It’s a lot scarier putting the suit on than it is actually putting your hands on the line. But, in essence, you’re working at the same potential — the same voltage environment as the transmission line.” Garza said there was only one company able and willing to take on the project — Houston-based . It agreed to do the work by December 2026, shortening the original 2029 timeline. ERCOT had but CPS wasn’t able to find a contractor able to reach ERCOT’s desired deadline of next summer. ERCOT anticipates to improve the crowded circuit by adding more transmission capacity starting at a Leon Creek substation to be completed by the summer of 2027. “They have a unique skill set to package a number of their companies together to be able to deliver that line,” Garza said of Quanta. The complete circuit, which makes a ring around San Antonio, is at risk of failure when plentiful power from South Texas wind farms and batteries is needed in North Texas, where capacity hasn’t kept up with increasing demand. Overloading the circuit to meet that demand could prompt grid-wide cascading outages." ($) " Houston clocks record overtime pay for police, fire as city contends with $330M deficit,” Houston Chronicle's Abby Church — “Despite Houston’s efforts to fill vacancies in its and , city leaders continue to shell out more and more cash to pay their employees overtime. As Houston contends with a heading into the next fiscal year, top officials with the controller’s office said Tuesday that Houston had . The city budgeted around $65 million for employee overtime in last year’s budget, a figure that represents 4% of the city’s personnel budget. Houston is on track to spend more than double the budgeted amount, and may consequentially spend close to $137 million on overtime alone before the end of the fiscal year, Deputy Controller Will Jones told council members at Tuesday’s Budget and Fiscal Affairs meeting. The city’s solid waste, police and fire departments have all taken several steps to hire more personnel, from increasing pay to adding another police cadet class to passing a historic deal with the fire union that includes $650 million in back pay. Even so, overtime pay continues to increase. Some council members listened to the presentation Tuesday with furrowed brows, and Mayor Pro Tem Martha Castex-Tatum referred to the information as “bleak.” Council Member Sallie Alcorn , the committee’s chair, had only one remark after she thanked Jones for the presentation. “I learned a lot,” she said. “My stomach hurts.” The three culprits of increased overtime spending are Houston’s Solid Waste Management, Fire and Police departments. According to data presented Tuesday, the police department was budgeted $13.7 million in overtime pay, but is on track to spend nearly $40 million – a 190% increase over its budget. The fire department, while budgeted for $45.3 million in overtime, is on track to spend $88 million – a 94% increase over budget. While Solid Waste was only budgeted $4 million in overtime, the department is on track to instead spend $7.1 million – 75% over its budget, according to data presented Tuesday. Truck operators were the most frequent users of overtime, and the position happens to be the hardest to fill at the department, Jones said. The overtime use at Solid Waste comes despite increased spending on contracting outside vendors to pick up trash and adding new vehicles. In 2024, missed trash pickup was the citywide. An analysis of the fire and police department’s top earners showed firefighters were on average making 231% of their pay from overtime alone, while police officers made 118% of their pay from overtime. The same analysis for Solid Waste showed top earners made on average 91% of their salary from overtime. All three departments were impacted by spikes in overtime during the derecho in May, Hurricane Beryl and January’s winter storm, but even without those natural disasters, all three departments would still be over their yearly overtime budgets, Jones said. A Solid Waste crew picks up recycling on Allston Street in June. Solid Waste workers are among the municipal workers that will get 9 percent raises over the next three months, after the city approved a labor deal with its employees." ($) | 2026 “Tarrant County Democratic Party lays off all paid staff, declines to share details, ” Fort Worth Report'sCecilia Lenzen — "Tarrant County Democratic Party Chair Crystal Gayden said Tuesday that her party is strategically restructuring, although she declined to share details on what that restructuring looks like. Lon Burnam , a Democrat who represented Tarrant County in the Texas House between 1997 and 2015 and remains involved with the party, confirmed that the party has laid off its three paid staff positions. The layoffs were first made public in the newsletter. Gayden declined to confirm how many staffers were laid off, when the layoffs were implemented or how they stand to impact the party. She said the party is currently focused on in the Tarrant County Commissioners Court. “The Tarrant County Democratic Party’s recent restructuring is a strategic decision, not a financial crisis,” Gayden, an attorney and previous judicial candidate who has served as chair since 2023, said in a statement to the Report. “It is common for parties to adjust staffing between election cycles.” Several other leaders within the party declined to comment, referring questions to Gayden. The layoffs come one month before the , when City Council, school board and other local races will be on the ballot across Tarrant County. Local races are all nonpartisan positions, but both the county’s Democrat and Republican parties have become increasingly involved in recent years. Burnam said he’s disappointed by the layoffs but noted that it’s common for political parties to struggle with fundraising after a presidential election. “Let’s be clear that it is always the case that after any presidential election, it is hard to raise money for either party — but it’s particularly hard if you lose,” Burnam said. President Joe Biden narrowly won Tarrant County in the 2020 presidential election, becoming the first Democrat to do so since Lyndon B. Johnson’s victory in 1964. The county in 2024, supporting President Donald Trump with 51.82% of the vote. Tarrant Republicans are celebrating the layoffs as a victory. Bo French , chair of the Tarrant County Republican Party, was outspoken before and after the November election about his goal to make the county “inhospitable to Democrats” through more involvement in local races. In response to the layoffs Tuesday, French posted on X that his “plan is working.” In an emailed statement to the Report, French said the Democratic Party’s members “continue to take the 20% position on 80-20 issues.” “It’s no wonder they are having trouble raising money,” he said." | NEWS FROM AROUND THE STATE AAS: "Texas bill would require citizenship proof to vote. Why rights groups say it’s dangerous. " AAS: "What is a faculty senate? As Texas lawmakers seek to regulate them, some defend its merit " AAS: "Here 's why school nurses need Texas law change to give new epi nasal spray to students" HOU CHRON: "Houston quietly removes major bike lane protections, sparking outrage " AAS: "Matthew McConaughey asks Texas Senate panel to support film incentives " HOU CHRON: "Judge pauses Trump administration plans to end temporary legal protections for Venezuelans " KXAN: "Matthew McConaughey and other stars push Senate to make Texas next film haven " THE TEXAN: "Superior HealthPlan Launches Internal Investigation After DOGE Hearing and CEO’s Firing, per Staff Meeting Audio " TPR: "Matthew McConaughey, industry reps push for Texas lawmakers to beef up state 's film incentive fund" MY RGV: "Texas school libraries could have tighter oversight if Republicans get their way " HOU CHRON: "Houston group wants to recall Mayor John Whitmire. name: Dan Sullivan
subject: My work isn’t done
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alaskans for Dan Sullivan](https://e
body:
I officially announced my reelection campaign for U.S. Senate here in Alaska

| | |
---
[](https://e.realamericanreporting.com/rd/9z2zbi0lk4c4mjsqu2e4cg2om5lilfq7i1a49u6pjro_rp22sh2s8i6eob6cdi68ob4c3h2bs)
---
Last night, I officially announced my reelection campaign for U.S. Senate here in Alaska!
---
[](https://e.realamericanreporting.com/rd/9z2zldqoq0efffqv9sjelarv2mmoo52h1bie49mji6g_rp22sh2s8i6eob6cdi68ob4c3h2bs)
---
When I first ran in 2014, it was because I believed Alaska needed a stronger voice in Washington, D.C. Our challenges and opportunities are different than the rest of the country, and we needed someone who would boldly fight for them. **Over the last 12 years, I have worked to:** ✩ **__Unleash__ Alaskan energy** ✩ **__Rebuild__ __ our military****✩ _Fight_ for our veterans** **✩ __Create__ good-paying jobs for our hard-working families****✩ _Secure_ our southern border** While I believe we've made tremendous strides forward, Joe Biden was a disaster for Alaska's prosperity. His administration imposed 70 overreaching executive orders with a single goal: **to lock up Alaska. ** I’m proud to have led the fight against the Biden administration every step of the way. Working with President Trump’s strong leadership, we’ve already helped “lift the sanctions on Alaska. Last month, Trump **took action** with one of the very first measures of his second term, when he signed an executive order to unlock Alaska’s “extraordinary resource potential.”**

____[____Today, I am asking for your help to jumpstart my re-election campaign so we can continue working to unlock Alaska's potential.____](https://e.realamericanreporting.com/rd/9z2zt9l9p18mlu8okajodi6l336um9vqqr19hdhrdmg_rp22sh2s8i6eob6cdi68ob4c3h2bs)____**
---
[](https://e.realamericanreporting.com/rd/9z2zs4u6ff8jjuvoj4fq9tm9u2j1ngp1u2rvj1mt5j0_rp22sh2s8i6eob6cdi68ob4c3h2bs)
---
What I find concerning is that keeping our state on lockdown isn't just a Biden administration policy, but a major item in the Democrats' playbook. From shutting down our energy potential to controlling our land – there's no question they will spend millions of dollars to flip this U.S. Senate seat to push their **anti-Alaska agenda. ** **They know what I stand for.

_They know that I will always fight for Alaska and our way of life. _** This reelection race comes at a pivotal time. Republicans have control of the Senate, but only by a hair. Expanding our majority is necessary to ensure President Trump's America First policies are supported by Congress. _That's how we usher in the Golden Age of America. _ As it stands right now, 22 Republican seats are on the line in 2026. I know that Chuck Schumer and his extreme allies are sure to seize every opportunity they can to try and flip the Senate back into their control – **and Alaska will be in their crosshairs. ** ****[****If you have my back, please help me jumpstart my reelection campaign with a donation of $5, $15, $20.26, or any amount up to $7,000 today!****](https://e.realamericanreporting.com/rd/9z2z64k2pn34ud2lq55e7j6sthhpccak0k9b5m1hpsg_rp22sh2s8i6eob6cdi68ob4c3h2bs)****
---
Sincerely,
**Dan Sullivan
** _U.S. Senator, Alaska_
---
[](https://e.realamericanreporting.com/rd/9z2zgk3ps4i1kbntal0rrn0vngjbbd023hadls24llo_rp22sh2s8i6eob6cdi68ob4c3h2bs)
---

---

I know that Chuck Schumer and his extreme allies are sure to seize every opportunity they can to try and flip the Senate back into their control – and Alaska will be in their crosshairs. ** Sincerely, Dan Sullivan U.S. Senator, Alaska | | | ** | | | | | Dan Sullivan is a retired member of the U.S. Marine Corps. name: Congresswoman Sarah Elfreth
subject: The Last 24 Hours
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Elfreth for Maryland
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/blu-sefc-q1-2025-em?refcode=BLU_EM_SEFC_2025-01-04_1&amount=25 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/blu-sefc-q1-2025-em?refcode=BLU_EM_SEFC_2025-01-04_1&amount=25]
Yesterday was a good day for our democratic republic:
🇺🇸 Senator Cory Booker held the Senate floor for a record 25 hours and 5 minutes, speaking to the shared responsibility of every American to defend our Constitution.
🇺🇸 A number of courageous Republicans joined Democrats in a procedural vote that could pave the way for brand new moms and dads in Congress to vote remotely.
🇺🇸 In Wisconsin, voters rejected extremism and million-dollar bribes in filling a seat on their Supreme Court.
[https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXfGiRJwHike9zriXEbMAv4FjczurjbPVrKb1jnaqs4bqjYaaPtPkfhiHzEcPxP7q7S7NoWH-RSvqtLsLcKJv64rSRtjhw6ZYhxLo7RTLKZVmX6ZQDD6VI6JaqqY0Uk4HvSdMbri?key=FnX8lhuNCjSiCFOcYVEApoUR]
These are small but mighty wins, especially after the chaos of the last few months. This quick photo I took while leaving the Capitol last night captures the optimism I have – and I hope we can all hold onto – in the strength and resilience of this nation.
As Senator Booker said in his speech, we must all lean in and stay in this fight. Will you help us build on this momentum? [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/blu-sefc-q2-2025-em?refcode=blu_em_sefc_2025-04-02]

Thank you,
Sarah Elfreth

name: Cory Booker
subject: My remarks on the Senate floor
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY SCHIFF FOR SENATE
body:
| This is a moment where we must all speak up.
[](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1014?c0=abs-em-booker25.gMTtR1&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_TAN_GEN_AC90_20250402_AM1_V1_S1&email=&t=3&akid=8425%2E5257348%2ERjhy8O)
Derek, it’s Cory.
Yesterday, I stepped off the Senate floor after speaking for over 25 hours straight — because I cannot stand idly by while Donald Trump and Elon Musk completely disregard the rule of law, the Constitution, and the needs of the American people.
I may be tired and a little hoarse, but as I said again and again in the Senate: this is a moment where we cannot afford to be silent. We must speak up.
What’s clear to me is that this is just the beginning. Americans across this country, no matter their title or party, are ready to be heard.
I believe that history will show we rose to meet this moment.
It will show that we did not let the chaos and division go unanswered.
It will show that when our president chose to spread lies and sow fear, we chose to come together, to work together, and to rise together.
[**If you’re willing and able, support our movement for justice, opportunity, and freedom by splitting a donation between me and Adam Schiff today.**](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1014?c0=abs-em-booker25.gMTtR1&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_TAN_GEN_AC90_20250402_AM1_V1_S1&email=&t=4&akid=8425%2E5257348%2ERjhy8O)

name: Team Risch
subject: Senator Risch has President Trump’sEndorsement – How about YOURS?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the NRSC
body:
**_["Senator Jim Risch has my Complete and Total Endorsement for Re-Election, he is an exceptional man, and HE WILL NEVER LET YOU DOWN!"](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zguo86df7fs6broeu7k8q00qfuknbpsk0321qjq8_rp22sh2s8i6eob6cdi6coj6clh2bs)_

[\-- President Donald J. Trump](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zt781nkl08fd625ulv715np5pg2gk2fl8kvl26e0_rp22sh2s8i6eob6cdi6coj6clh2bs)**

Friend,

**[President Donald J. Trump](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2z5bafql23c7pu6afcm9b59ffr62vuoj52qkcnfv0_rp22sh2s8i6eob6cdi6coj6clh2bs) **recently reiterated his rock-solid endorsement of Senator Jim Risch.

President Trump knows that Jim Risch is a huge part of our effort to roll back the disastrous Biden-Harris Democrat agenda in Washington, D.C., and we simply can't afford to lose his leadership in the Senate.

**[Here's how the Commander-in-Chief put it himself:](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zg31ucplo84onou538v0p3uqr543land4jb1lsto_rp22sh2s8i6eob6cdi6coj6clh2bs)**

_**["As the Chair of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, Jim is fighting tirelessly to advance our America First Agenda.

He is working hard to Grow our Economy, Create GREAT Jobs, Strongly Support our Farmers and Ranchers, Champion American Energy DOMINANCE, Secure our Border, Strengthen our Military/Veterans, Defend our always under siege Second Amendment, and Restore PEACE THROUGH STRENGTH..."](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zl5q41ihnslssfok8usi0dq5aci542mdi6nmlg60_rp22sh2s8i6eob6cdi6coj6clh2bs)**_

See what we mean?

President Trump knows we need Jim Risch, his leadership, and his energy for making America greater than ever right where he belongs -- representing the great State of Idaho in his crucial U.S. Senate role.

That's why we hope you'll step forward now, friend, and **[make YOUR own endorsement of Senator Risch's re-election effort by making a generous contribution](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zf0q0um8ghuiodnnfl88qarq35ff6m73uurcij70_rp22sh2s8i6eob6cdi6coj6clh2bs)** to help him win this pivotal re-election battle in the Gem State and keep our critical GOP Senate Majority on Capitol Hill.

Friend, we must make sure that Team Risch has **[the resources we need to get our winning message to the voters.](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zc7od73fa75cc3nsrb2f8hk1pmjkemhk8aq5tmn0_rp22sh2s8i6eob6cdi6coj6clh2bs)**

We need you to make a difference, friend -- **[will you show your support for Senator Risch today?](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2z7hgn3juq13i6v175g59jhp8jniq92oe52343neo_rp22sh2s8i6eob6cdi6coj6clh2bs)**

_Thank you! _

Team Risch

**[Add your Endorsement to the Growing List TODAY>>](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2z6f2pifnbpoe7ii5bapva3l6d33qikmpfihsm1a8_rp22sh2s8i6eob6cdi6coj6clh2bs)**

subject: It's easy for kids to forget why we celebrate Easter

| Peter, The Easter season is here! That means it's the perfect time to help your kids remember why we celebrate Easter: The ultimate sacrifice that Jesus made, and His glorious resurrection! If your kids are like most, they look forward to Easter morning with eager anticipation! The joy of receiving baskets of goodies and sweet treats is enough to make any child excited. But with all the excitement, it's easy for kids to forget why we celebrate Easter: The resurrection of Jesus Christ.
---
| [ ](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=802891b9d8fd4718a0738bfe2d35518a&_e=urRMzfNSUBwwFmRNEF3FBbGbD786LvnR82SEn_o4xrVxlXg1mSKej7j72y63-Xwt8VwuZPFZElx-RmRLJdp6MDL9NK5-u-czHZzaDNz9yuCRmVk5UkzC3Mojc2NlVrnIhzDtVoA-Hn2rD_Ygeo33H9TQVFWJ3-G5IwcnWSRhkKikh-iYrY88vQqd3-oUQd7gyVOJj-_q_bVrMFwsSm2jhzL_ZE1Z8dFzhWrm9JcqJ6XWeV0BAahYrSnsi5RVu-AL2LCdzMrPDogbFLFYjoTadJKDmiyMMrvhD_GUQPWen3KdhPyIv64hoN7n_adPqceGlxq_o22qaHSNfMl4nQNEI69r6oWz5_GDaigtfQw1bOxedPpbi24duRAl1gUbd6nGz7-DRH8UsU4uaAbnnO1ibQ%3D%3D)
---
|
---
That's why we're giving away The Kids Guide to the Bible: The Resurrection of Jesus as part of this free bundle offer for Easter! This special gift is packed with pages of colorful illustrations and important Biblical facts about how and why Jesus died for our sins, and how He was resurrected from the dead. You'll also receive the latest issue of EverBright Kids Magazine & Activity Book for free. You just need to pay $1 s&p; each, which is a small price to help your kids remember that Jesus is the reason for the season! Have a very happy and blessed Easter, Your friends at The Kids Guide to The Bible

name: Dan Sullivan
subject: My work isn’t done
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alaskans for Dan Sullivan](https://e
body:
I officially announced my reelection campaign for U.S. Senate here in Alaska

| | |
---
[](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zcm99fhf0b02pvnejpj94ggon41u1usl2a701hn8_rp22sh2s8i6eob6cdi6coj1c9h2bs)
---
Last night, I officially announced my reelection campaign for U.S. Senate here in Alaska!
---
[](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zl6eftiotd9igbucfm2ddmpps33q5k5nkiqh6pr0_rp22sh2s8i6eob6cdi6coj1c9h2bs)
---
When I first ran in 2014, it was because I believed Alaska needed a stronger voice in Washington, D.C. Our challenges and opportunities are different than the rest of the country, and we needed someone who would boldly fight for them. **Over the last 12 years, I have worked to:** ✩ **__Unleash__ Alaskan energy** ✩ **__Rebuild__ __ our military****✩ _Fight_ for our veterans** **✩ __Create__ good-paying jobs for our hard-working families****✩ _Secure_ our southern border** While I believe we've made tremendous strides forward, Joe Biden was a disaster for Alaska's prosperity. His administration imposed 70 overreaching executive orders with a single goal: **to lock up Alaska. ** I’m proud to have led the fight against the Biden administration every step of the way. Working with President Trump’s strong leadership, we’ve already helped “lift the sanctions on Alaska. Last month, Trump **took action** with one of the very first measures of his second term, when he signed an executive order to unlock Alaska’s “extraordinary resource potential.”**

____[____Today, I am asking for your help to jumpstart my re-election campaign so we can continue working to unlock Alaska's potential.____](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zcmg9lcmk80vvp0015ghjei8sk00g072k589s9lg_rp22sh2s8i6eob6cdi6coj1c9h2bs)____**
---
[](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zjsh4ebp8jbo3vv6lg2tpah0l8lsrha457dn4sjo_rp22sh2s8i6eob6cdi6coj1c9h2bs)
---
What I find concerning is that keeping our state on lockdown isn't just a Biden administration policy, but a major item in the Democrats' playbook. From shutting down our energy potential to controlling our land – there's no question they will spend millions of dollars to flip this U.S. Senate seat to push their **anti-Alaska agenda. ** **They know what I stand for.

_They know that I will always fight for Alaska and our way of life. _** This reelection race comes at a pivotal time. Republicans have control of the Senate, but only by a hair. Expanding our majority is necessary to ensure President Trump's America First policies are supported by Congress. _That's how we usher in the Golden Age of America. _ As it stands right now, 22 Republican seats are on the line in 2026. I know that Chuck Schumer and his extreme allies are sure to seize every opportunity they can to try and flip the Senate back into their control – **and Alaska will be in their crosshairs. ** ****[****If you have my back, please help me jumpstart my reelection campaign with a donation of $5, $15, $20.26, or any amount up to $7,000 today!****](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zruvo9ddh86o4usvtpa16bqec8577insed5tf45g_rp22sh2s8i6eob6cdi6coj1c9h2bs)****
---
Sincerely,
**Dan Sullivan
** _U.S. Senator, Alaska_
---

clean_body: I officially announced my reelection campaign for U.S. Senate here in Alaska | | | Last night, I officially announced my reelection campaign for U.S. Senate here in Alaska! When I first ran in 2014, it was because I believed Alaska needed a stronger voice in Washington, D.C. Our challenges and opportunities are different than the rest of the country, and we needed someone who would boldly fight for them. Over the last 12 years, I have worked to: ✩ Unleash Alaskan energy ✩ Rebuild our military✩ Fight for our veterans ✩ Create good-paying jobs for our hard-working families✩ Secure our southern border While I believe we've made tremendous strides forward, Joe Biden was a disaster for Alaska's prosperity. His administration imposed 70 overreaching executive orders with a single goal: to lock up Alaska. I’m proud to have led the fight against the Biden administration every step of the way. Working with President Trump’s strong leadership, we’ve already helped “lift the sanctions on Alaska. Last month, Trump took action with one of the very first measures of his second term, when he signed an executive order to unlock Alaska’s “extraordinary resource potential.” __ What I find concerning is that keeping our state on lockdown isn't just a Biden administration policy, but a major item in the Democrats' playbook. From shutting down our energy potential to controlling our land – there's no question they will spend millions of dollars to flip this U.S. Senate seat to push their anti-Alaska agenda. They know what I stand for. They know that I will always fight for Alaska and our way of life. This reelection race comes at a pivotal time. Republicans have control of the Senate, but only by a hair. Expanding our majority is necessary to ensure President Trump's America First policies are supported by Congress. That's how we usher in the Golden Age of America. As it stands right now, 22 Republican seats are on the line in 2026. I know that Chuck Schumer and his extreme allies are sure to seize every opportunity they can to try and flip the Senate back into their control – and Alaska will be in their crosshairs. ** Sincerely, Dan Sullivan U.S. Senator, Alaska | | | ** | | | | | Dan Sullivan is a retired member of the U.S. Marine Corps. Use of his military rank, job titles, and photographs in uniform does not imply endorsement by the US Marine Corps or the Department of Defense. name: Senator Jim Risch (R-ID)
subject: Thank you, Mr. President
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the NRSC
body:
Friend,

It's an honor to have **[President Trump's](https://e.trumptrainnews.com/rd/9z2zd660623576gouqmtu0dtrhiopt2hn2ji3fk1vog_rp22sh2s8i6eob6cdi6coj6c3h2bs)** endorsement in this pivotal U.S. Senate re-election fight to expand our critical new America First GOP Majority in the U.S. Senate!

And since I can't say it any better than our Commander-in-Chief, I'll let him speak for himself -- right here in his tremendous endorsement:

[](https://e.trumptrainnews.com/rd/9z2zsgc53lougrlhnd17isr9203mprjm9rq9t16qmso_rp22sh2s8i6eob6cdi6coj6c3h2bs)

I know what President Trump knows -- that we can't take anything for granted in this Midterm Reelection race -- and that maintaining our indispensable GOP Senate Majority throughout President Trump's full term starts with maintaining the seats we already hold.

So I'm not at all surprised he's getting out in front of the rapidly approaching Midterm Election with his crucial endorsement of my campaign **[\-- and now I need YOU to join him!](https://e.trumptrainnews.com/rd/9z2z4k6kr2fhhnq7t60us1iom2tbe9c4t7t6467mvhg_rp22sh2s8i6eob6cdi6coj6c3h2bs)**

Will you **[give me YOUR Endorsement in this Idaho U.S. Senate race right now?](https://e.trumptrainnews.com/rd/9z2ztsulfh1heft988ed7qepb6f6gsiqo2s0uvgn48o_rp22sh2s8i6eob6cdi6coj6c3h2bs) **

**[Your key support at this early stage](https://e.trumptrainnews.com/rd/9z2zd8gke61u35dtghebkmdhhpr5mqhqrj55k1jj7ag_rp22sh2s8i6eob6cdi6coj6c3h2bs)** will go a long way toward ensuring we can lay the foundation for success and get our winning message of commonsense conservative principles and policies to the voters who will decide.

_Thank you,_ friend, for your continued support of our shared Conservative values.

Sincerely,
[](https://e.trumptrainnews.com/rd/9z2z4nd31i0clgn8ljkm3ekiketf9fjr3rmmv8datc8_rp22sh2s8i6eob6cdi6coj6c3h2bs)
Jim Risch

[ENDORSE JIM RISCH >>](https://e.trumptrainnews.com/rd/9z2zetkso936f7cundl8t7dcmpqcnbo5b78us466ssg_rp22sh2s8i6eob6cdi6coj6c3h2bs)

name: Kamala Harris
subject: Cory Booker
disclaimer_text: paid for by cutting programs like Medicaid, Medicare, Social Security, Head Start, nutrition programs for kids in school and more
body:
Last night, my friend Cory Booker showed us what it means to fight for the people of this country.

Peter --
Last night, my friend Cory Booker showed us what it means to fight for the people of this country.
He stood in the Senate -- for 25 hours straight -- and talked about the damage the Trump administration and the Republican Party is doing to our country.
He talked about their plan to cut taxes for billionaires paid for by cutting programs like Medicaid, Medicare, Social Security, Head Start, nutrition programs for kids in school and more.
He talked about this administration's disregard for the Constitution and the rule of law.
And he talked about how the filibuster record he broke was set by a man trying to stop the passage of the Civil Rights Act.
Cory Booker stood for us -- to show the way forward in the fights ahead -- and now he needs us to stand up for him in his re-election.
[**Can you please split a $5 contribution to Cory Booker's campaign and the Democratic National Committee so they have the resources needed to carry this fight forward.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/booker-dnc?refcode=em250402-1&refcode2=492_2563979_jnQkrt&akid=492%2E2563979%2EjnQkrt)

name: Dan Sullivan
subject: My work isn’t done
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alaskans for Dan Sullivan](https://e
body:
I officially announced my reelection campaign for U.S. Senate here in Alaska

| | |
---
[](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2zth96an8vs4ahkd5t0e8j09hrg83hma9j6f2m088_rp22sh2s8i6eob6cdi6ao36c9h2bs)
---
Last night, I officially announced my reelection campaign for U.S. Senate here in Alaska!
---
[](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2zs1ps88q3am7h4ksp6n0huv0eh111h05pvuf9cao_rp22sh2s8i6eob6cdi6ao36c9h2bs)
---
When I first ran in 2014, it was because I believed Alaska needed a stronger voice in Washington, D.C. Our challenges and opportunities are different than the rest of the country, and we needed someone who would boldly fight for them. **Over the last 12 years, I have worked to:** ✩ **__Unleash__ Alaskan energy** ✩ **__Rebuild__ __ our military****✩ _Fight_ for our veterans** **✩ __Create__ good-paying jobs for our hard-working families****✩ _Secure_ our southern border** While I believe we've made tremendous strides forward, Joe Biden was a disaster for Alaska's prosperity. His administration imposed 70 overreaching executive orders with a single goal: **to lock up Alaska. ** I’m proud to have led the fight against the Biden administration every step of the way. Working with President Trump’s strong leadership, we’ve already helped “lift the sanctions on Alaska. Last month, Trump **took action** with one of the very first measures of his second term, when he signed an executive order to unlock Alaska’s “extraordinary resource potential.”**

____[____Today, I am asking for your help to jumpstart my re-election campaign so we can continue working to unlock Alaska's potential.____](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2z71j3i54dosu8mml9k0ljm0u8bd5jbbt0pcstge0_rp22sh2s8i6eob6cdi6ao36c9h2bs)____**
---
[](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2z2ucsj14j4no93gk4fafj20nrj1n27ro1gp5qne8_rp22sh2s8i6eob6cdi6ao36c9h2bs)
---
What I find concerning is that keeping our state on lockdown isn't just a Biden administration policy, but a major item in the Democrats' playbook. From shutting down our energy potential to controlling our land – there's no question they will spend millions of dollars to flip this U.S. Senate seat to push their **anti-Alaska agenda. ** **They know what I stand for.

_They know that I will always fight for Alaska and our way of life. _** This reelection race comes at a pivotal time. Republicans have control of the Senate, but only by a hair. Expanding our majority is necessary to ensure President Trump's America First policies are supported by Congress. _That's how we usher in the Golden Age of America. _ As it stands right now, 22 Republican seats are on the line in 2026. I know that Chuck Schumer and his extreme allies are sure to seize every opportunity they can to try and flip the Senate back into their control – **and Alaska will be in their crosshairs. ** ****[****If you have my back, please help me jumpstart my reelection campaign with a donation of $5, $15, $20.26, or any amount up to $7,000 today!****](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2z5l5u30a66nlgrsvm12afcrd4uhidba4lna6hguo_rp22sh2s8i6eob6cdi6ao36c9h2bs)****
---
Sincerely,
**Dan Sullivan
** _U.S. Senator, Alaska_
---
[](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2znuqds78edd3d37gkj5vot7l4fdfjl04lhvfnfjg_rp22sh2s8i6eob6cdi6ao36c9h2bs)
---
[](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2zrqepdj375gkcmqg08dgt5vrjho764lbtk11i9fo_rp22sh2s8i6eob6cdi6ao36c9h2bs)
---

---
|
---
| | [](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2znhn9a6m2c4tr0pirks1nl9g4mkdpsbjv7rh8t1g_rp22sh2s8i6eob6cdi6ao36c9h2bs)
---
[](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2zsngknvkh0l7l5ft110vtj8ipjme7j4rrvvgv2e0_rp22sh2s8i6eob6cdi6ao36c9h2bs)
---
[](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2zbm00bd24v67an7j1p4929c176ufjpdkt70a0tfo_rp22sh2s8i6eob6cdi6ao36c9h2bs)
---
****

subject: Senator Risch has President Trump’sEndorsement – How about YOURS?

body:
**_["Senator Jim Risch has my Complete and Total Endorsement for Re-Election, he is an exceptional man, and HE WILL NEVER LET YOU DOWN!"](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2znvijlcse3jidb0t8oibc2dn01e6lqpvlbcn3su8_rp22sh2s8i6eob6cdi6ao36c9h2bs)_

[\-- President Donald J. Trump](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2ze578cuorg73pb9gbpslivtd1vm7207k8kbj7hvg_rp22sh2s8i6eob6cdi6ao36c9h2bs)**

Friend,

**[President Donald J. Trump](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zod9dnslrm0madp83umvd7rg9ptp3g1jqkbbv048_rp22sh2s8i6eob6cdi6ao36c9h2bs) **recently reiterated his rock-solid endorsement of Senator Jim Risch.

President Trump knows that Jim Risch is a huge part of our effort to roll back the disastrous Biden-Harris Democrat agenda in Washington, D.C., and we simply can't afford to lose his leadership in the Senate.

**[Here's how the Commander-in-Chief put it himself:](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z8p5lknef5uv83me834uhp28p7863or7a75v7dj0_rp22sh2s8i6eob6cdi6ao36c9h2bs)**

_**["As the Chair of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, Jim is fighting tirelessly to advance our America First Agenda.

He is working hard to Grow our Economy, Create GREAT Jobs, Strongly Support our Farmers and Ranchers, Champion American Energy DOMINANCE, Secure our Border, Strengthen our Military/Veterans, Defend our always under siege Second Amendment, and Restore PEACE THROUGH STRENGTH..."](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zpadk809fhmne99j3dt6p91lcfp0du44q11h48b8_rp22sh2s8i6eob6cdi6ao36c9h2bs)**_

See what we mean?

President Trump knows we need Jim Risch, his leadership, and his energy for making America greater than ever right where he belongs -- representing the great State of Idaho in his crucial U.S. Senate role.

That's why we hope you'll step forward now, friend, and **[make YOUR own endorsement of Senator Risch's re-election effort by making a generous contribution](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zh9m5qbpaioghv4jdt744d60gjgpkqi9svbf4nt8_rp22sh2s8i6eob6cdi6ao36c9h2bs)** to help him win this pivotal re-election battle in the Gem State and keep our critical GOP Senate Majority on Capitol Hill.

Friend, we must make sure that Team Risch has **[the resources we need to get our winning message to the voters.](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zhv5fvt8ataooecc6jgdef029415qgoabl4ih5go_rp22sh2s8i6eob6cdi6ao36c9h2bs)**

We need you to make a difference, friend -- **[will you show your support for Senator Risch today?](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z7r1055h9llbd0q8nhk09s76i5uqgr9plkprjjm0_rp22sh2s8i6eob6cdi6ao36c9h2bs)**

_Thank you! _

Team Risch

**[Add your Endorsement to the Growing List TODAY>>](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z6b01i9a9191a0p2f0d6jvc1k8b5k5ohv8ionn88_rp22sh2s8i6eob6cdi6ao36c9h2bs)**

subject: Last Night’s Results Show Democrats the Path Forward
body:
| | | | | [](https://collegedems.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b3a52587106e5f265f227782b&id=9e16bc9849&e=d6c5c7d7ec)
